unverified.label: The Return to the Age of Research
unverified.quote: But now the scale is so big. Is the belief really, 'Oh, it’s so big, but if you had 100x more, everything would be so different?'... I don’t think that’s true. So it’s back to the age of research again, just with big computers.

unverified.label: Value Functions for RL Efficiency
unverified.quote: The value function lets you short-circuit the wait until the very end... I think anything you can do with a value function, you can do without, just more slowly.

unverified.label: Peak Data
unverified.quote: pre-training as we know it will unquestionably end... because while computers growing through better Hardware better algorithms and logic clusters... the data is not growing because we have but one internet

unverified.label: Pre-training Learns the World, Fine-Tuning Specifies Behavior

all_results.label: Scaling Deep Neural Networks Replicates Cognition
all_results.quote: if the human brain can do something, then a big artificial neural network could do something similar too.

all_results.label: The Return to the Age of Research
all_results.quote: But now the scale is so big. Is the belief really, 'Oh, it’s so big, but if you had 100x more, everything would be so different?'... I don’t think that’s true. So it’s back to the age of research again, just with big computers.

all_results.label: AGI as a Continual Learner
all_results.quote: I produce a superintelligent 15-year-old that’s very eager to go. They don’t know very much at all, a great student, very eager. You go and be a programmer, you go and be a doctor, go and learn.

all_results.label: Next-Word Prediction Learns Reality
all_results.quote: what the neural network learns is some representation of the process that produced the text. This text is actually a projection of the world

all_results.label: Prediction Drives Understanding
all_results.quote: if you can make really good guesses as to what's going to come next, you can't make it perfectly, it's impossible. But if you can make a really good guess, you need to have a meaningful degree of understanding.

all_results.label: Prediction is Compression
all_results.quote: To predict the data well, to compress it well, you need to understand more and more about the world that produced the data.

all_results.label: The Generalization Gap
all_results.quote: The thing which I think is the most fundamental is that these models somehow just generalize dramatically worse than people. It’s super obvious.

all_results.label: The Scaling Hypothesis
all_results.quote: The thing that the GPTs have shown in particular is that scaling requires that you increase the compute and the data in tandem at the same time.

all_results.label: Value Functions for RL Efficiency
all_results.quote: The value function lets you short-circuit the wait until the very end... I think anything you can do with a value function, you can do without, just more slowly.

all_results.label: Avoid Hardcoding
all_results.quote: it just seems unlikely to me that we will be smart enough to be able to hard-code things for the truly difficult problems

all_results.label: Conviction in Deep Learning
all_results.quote: Once you have a lot of rights data and a lot of compute, then there is a third thing which is needed as well, and that is conviction, conviction that if you take the right stuff... that it will, in fact work.

all_results.label: Focus Safety on Superintelligence, Not Current Models
all_results.quote: You can start perhaps worrying about the implications of the tools that we have today, and I think that it is a very valid thing to do, but that's not where I allocate my concern.

all_results.label: Industry Competitors Must Unite on Safety
all_results.quote: In the future, there will be much more challenging situations of this nature, and it will be critical for the relevant leaders to rise up to the occasion, for fierce competitors to put their differences aside.

all_results.label: Leverage Superintelligence for Alignment
all_results.quote: I do hope that at this point we will have the superintelligence itself try to help us solve the challenge in the world that it creates.

all_results.label: Parent-Child Relationship as Alignment Gold Standard
all_results.quote: In my opinion, this is the gold standard. It is a generally true statement that people really care about children.

all_results.label: Peak Data
all_results.quote: pre-training as we know it will unquestionably end... because while computers growing through better Hardware better algorithms and logic clusters... the data is not growing because we have but one internet

all_results.quote: what this does not do is specify the desired behavior that you wish our neural network to exhibit... this is where the fine tuning and the reinforcement learning from Human teachers [comes in]

all_results.label: Prioritize Safe Superintelligence Over Revenue
all_results.quote: first product will be the safe superintelligence, and it will not do anything else up until then

all_results.label: Proper Initialization Unlocks Optimization
all_results.quote: We show that gradient descent with momentum can train RNNs to solve problems with long-term dependencies, provided the RNNs are initialized properly and an appropriate momentum schedule is used.

all_results.label: Reasoning Breeds Unpredictability
all_results.quote: a system that reasons the more it reasons the more unpredictable it becomes

all_results.label: Scale What Matters
all_results.quote: I think the true statement is that it matters what you scale. Right now, we just found like a thing to scale that gives us something in return.

all_results.label: The Transition from Scaling to Research
all_results.quote: Is the belief that if you just 100x the scale, everything would be transformed? I don’t think that’s true. So it’s back to the age of research again, just with big computers.

all_results.label: Top-Down Research Taste
all_results.quote: The top-down belief is the thing that sustains you when the experiments contradict you. Because if you trust the data all the time, well sometimes you can be doing the correct thing but there's a bug.

all_results.label: Two-Stage AI Training (Pre-training + RLHF)
all_results.quote: You can say that the pre training process, and you just train a language model, you want to learn everything about the world, then the reinforcement learning from human feedback. Now we care about the outputs.

all_results.label: Domain Randomization
all_results.quote: if there is something in your simulation which you can't measure you randomized it and you require your policy to be able to solve it for any value of the randomization

all_results.label: Generative Sequence Modeling with RNNs
all_results.quote: An RNN defines a generative model over sequences if the loss function satisfies L(zt; yt) = − log p(yt; zt) for some parameterized family of distributions p(·; z) and if yt = vt+1.

all_results.quote: I'll mention three I see... I personally see three...

all_results.quote: It's beauty, simplicity, elegance, correct inspiration from the brain. All of those things need to be present at the same time. The more they are present, the more confident you can be in a top-down belief.
